pages.title: Priorities | Graham Stuart MP
pages.meta_description: My PrioritiesPeople are working hard, doing the right thing, and still feeling the squeeze. Backing local businesses helps keep jobs secure and prices down.
headings: h2: A New Deal for Young People
h2: Cost of living and household bills
h3: Graham Stuart MP for Beverley and Holderness
pages.text_excerpt: Priorities | Graham Stuart MP Skip to main content Graham Stuart MP Main navigation Home News Priorities My Views Street Surgeries About Me Contact Cost of Living Graham Stuart MP Priorities Tweet My Priorities People are working hard, doing the right thing, and still feeling the squeeze. Backing local businesses helps keep jobs secure and prices down. Creating real routes into work gives young people a future they can afford. Supporting farming and rural life protects British food and household bills. Fixing roads, improving NHS access, and tackling antisocial behaviour are about basic standards and fairness. I am not interested in slogans. I am interested in practical action that lowers costs, strengthens our communities, and delivers real results for Beverley and Holderness. Backing local businesses Keeping costs down for businesses helps keep prices down for families and protects local jobs. Backing businesses to keep prices down and jobs local. A New Deal for Young People Young people need real jobs they can live on, not schemes that lead nowhere. Real jobs so young people can afford to build a life. Cost of living and household bills Energy, food, council tax and travel costs are stretching working families. Relief for household bills. Fairness for working families. Rural life and farming British food production keeps prices stable and standards high. Backing British food to keep bills down and standards high. Roads and everyday infrastructure Poor infrastructure costs people money through repairs, fuel and lost time. Fixing what’s broken so it costs people less. NHS access, handled locally Getting timely care matters more than structures or slogans. Help when you need it, without paying more. Antisocial behaviour and standards People who do the right thing expect fair rules to be enforced. Fair rules, enforced fairly. pages.title: Street Surgeries | Graham Stuart MP
headings: h3: Graham Stuart MP for Beverley and Holderness
pages.text_excerpt: Street Surgeries | Graham Stuart MP Skip to main content Graham Stuart MP Main navigation Home News Priorities My Views Street Surgeries About Me Contact Cost of Living Graham Stuart MP Street Surgeries Tweet What’s a Street Surgery? My Street Surgeries take place outdoors in towns and villages across Beverley and Holderness on Saturday mornings. They provide an informal opportunity for you to meet me and share a problem, and also to ask about the work I am doing on your behalf. You don’t need to make an appointment and our conversation will be private if necessary. Street Surgery times (unless stated otherwise): Beverley: 10am – noon Hedon and Withernsea: 10.30am – noon Walkington, Bilton, Aldbrough, Keyingham, Cherry Burton and Woodmansey: 11am - noon Street Surgery dates: Saturday 9th May - Outside Aldi, Withernsea Saturday 13th June - Aldbrough Saturday 27th June - Keyingham Saturday 4th July - Toll Gavel, Beverley Saturday 5th September - Outside Aldi, Withernsea Saturday 19th September - Market Place, Hedon Saturday 17th October - Cherry Burton Saturday 31st October - Woodmansey Saturday 14th November - Market Place, Hedon Saturday 28th November - Outside Aldi, Withernsea Saturday 12th December - Toll Gavel, Beverley Newsletter signup Leave this field blank Get Graham's monthly newsletter Your email First name Last name Postcode Address ID Address 1 The information you provide is used in accordance with our Data Protection and Privacy Policy . By clicking this button you agree to your information being used in accordance with that policy. pages.title: About Me | Graham Stuart MP
headings: h2: Working Hard for Beverley & Holderness
h3: Graham Stuart MP for Beverley and Holderness
pages.text_excerpt: About Me | Graham Stuart MP Skip to main content Graham Stuart MP Main navigation Home News Priorities My Views Street Surgeries About Me Contact Cost of Living Graham Stuart MP About Me Tweet What I've Done You can read about what I've done for Beverley and Holderness at www.grahamstuart.com/whativedone . Some Facts About Me I was born in 1962 in Carlisle, the son of a doctor. I was educated at Glenalmond College before reading Philosophy and Law at Selwyn College, Cambridge from 1982-1985. I started my publishing business at the end of my first year at Cambridge. I was elected to Cambridge City Council in 1998, and served there until 2004. I stood for Parliament in Cambridge in 2001 before moving to Beverley in 2004. I was first elected as Beverley and Holderness's Member of Parliament in 2005. Political Positions 1998-2004 Cambridge City Councillor, Cherry Hinton Ward 2005-Present Member of Parliament, Beverley and Holderness 2006-2010 Member, Conservative Party Board 2010-2015 Chairman, House of Commons Education Selection Committee 2016-2018 Government Whip 2018-2021 Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State for International Trade 2022 Prime Minister's Trade Envoy to Vietnam 2022 Minister of State for Europe 2022-2024 Minister of State for Energy Security and Net Zero Interests Football, Cricket, Rugby, cycling, climate change, motorcycling, rural affairs, punk rock. Working Hard for Beverley & Holderness Come and say hello to me at my informal street surgeries across the constituency. I also welcome calls, letters and emails to my office, the contact details for which can be found in the 'Contact' section at the top of this page. You can also sign up to my e-newsletter which will allow you to keep up to date with all the local campaigns I am involved in. In the meantime, keep visiting http://www.grahamstuart.com/news for all the latest news. You can see expenses claimed to support my work representing Beverley and Holderness here https://www.theipsa.org.uk/mp-staffing-business-costs/your-mp/graham-stuart/1482 , About Me In Parliament My map About Beverley and Holderness Graham Stuart MP for Beverley and Holderness Footer About RSS Accessibility Cookies Privacy About Me In Parliament Promoted by Graham Stuart MP of 9 Cross St, Beverley HU17 9AX Copyright 2026 Graham Stuart MP.
